summary: cursed-modules exfiltrates installer secrets and provides remote command execution
details: Package declares preinstall, install, and postinstall lifecycle scripts all running install.js, which harvests hostname, full process.env,.npmrc contents, verdaccio configs, and files matching /flag/, then POSTs the data to webhook.site (a9d21bb7-9e09-446c-a85d-85f071ebcb82) and to 154.57.164.70:31682. On require, index.js runs an IIFE gated by a 12-hex-char Docker hostname pattern that queries the AWS instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254 for IAM credentials, calls aws sts get-caller-identity and secretsmanager listings, collects env and /flag files, and PUTs base64-encoded payloads to 154.57.164.70:32447 under module id ECT-654321. recon.js performs a parallel collection (env, /flag*, /etc/crontab, supervisord,.npmrc, HTB flag greps) and PUTs to 154.57.164.76:30728. rce.sh implements a two-module C2 relay: it PUTs commands to 154.57.164.70:32447/api/modules/ECT-654321 and GETs base64-encoded results from ECT-987654, giving the operator arbitrary command execution on installers. Payloads are base64-wrapped inside a fake `ecto_module` YAML manifest (power_level field) as a cover story. A shipped publish-and-arm.sh automates republishing and labels the package's role as dependency-confusion against a Verdaccio proxy.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
